parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: Read this email from your English teacher, Mr Novak, and the notes you have made.

stimulus.head: EMAIL
stimulus.body:
From: Mr Novak
Subject: New student arriving on Monday

Dear Class,

A new student called Alex is joining our class on Monday. Alex has just moved here from another country and doesn't know anyone yet.

I'm looking for a volunteer to help Alex during the first week. Would you like to do this? Tell me how you could help.

Mr Novak

bullets: offer to help Alex during the first week
say how you could help at school
suggest an activity to do with Alex after school
tasks.write: Write your email to Mr Novak.

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement in your school English-language magazine.

stimulus.title: HOW TO MAKE NEW FRIENDS
stimulus.body: Starting at a new school or club can be difficult. What is the best way to make new friends? Write an article giving your advice. The best articles will be published next month.

bullets: why making new friends can be difficult
your best advice for making friends
an example from your own experience

tasks.lead: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story.
tasks.startSentence: On her first day at the new school, Nadia sat down next to a girl with a friendly smile.

stimulus.body:
Your story must begin with this sentence:

On her first day at the new school, Nadia sat down next to a girl with a friendly smile.
